ORY FARMING C0NQUERNG DR0UTH TO REDUCE COST OF LIVING

Official of Dry Farming Con-
gress Tells of the Scope
and Significance of the
ACKNOWLEDGEMENTS How
It Has Benefited Farmers
the World Over.

pages.raw_text:
Secretary Of Dry Farming Congress
AS the invasion of civilization
has driven the old frontier of the
west backward and still backward
until it has disappearde in the
waves of the Pacific, so is the dry
farming movement, so called, fast
driving the drouth area and the
once existent, but somewhat mag
mary alkali plains from the Amer
lean map.


Fifteen years ago irrigation was
looked upon as theory scarcely


to be considered a commercial pos
sibility. The signing of $4,000, 900


bond law by the president of the


United States recently, definitely
proved what has in the intervening


years been worked out by the irri-
gationistS-not only the CONNER


cial possibilities, but the absolute


necessity of irrigation as a CONNER
cial asset in the west.


Five years ago dry farming, then
but little understood, although in
truth as old as the mountain ranges
of the glorious west, was derisively
received by all classes as being
land agents myth created as a
means of disposing of the acreage
included in the so called desert dis
tricts west of the eastern Colorado
line.


Today the Dry Farming congress
stands pre-eminent among agricul-
tural and civic development Organi-
cations in the world. Through it's
influence agricultural colleges have
added dry farming departments,
field men and demonstration farms
